code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test16-NOV-2016Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, indicating impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, ketonuria, glucosuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), suggesting possible nephrotic syndrome or acute kidney injury with tubular dysfunction. HbA1c is low, ruling out chronic hyperglycemia. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for underlying causes of renal impairment.
